chapters.summary: Council Staff reminds attendees to silence devices. Majority Leader Shaun Abreu welcomes members to the June 30, 2026 stated meeting, reminds the public that only council members may speak, and notes the silent approval gesture for support.
chapters.title: Majority Leader Abreu opens stated meeting and reviews decorum rules

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Ladies and gentlemen, at this time, please place all electronic devices, all electronic devices to vibrate.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Mr.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Majority Leader, it's all yours.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Good afternoon and welcome to the stated meeting of June 30, 2026.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: I am Majority Leader Shaun-Abreu, and I'd like to thank you for joining us.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: As a reminder, decorum must be respected at all times.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: If you would like to follow along, the agenda for today's meeting is posted on our website.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: On behalf of the body, I welcome the members of the public who have joined us today.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: During stated meetings, only members of the council may speak.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Members of the public shall remain silent.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: If you wish to express your support of any remarks, you may make the silent approval gesture.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Speaker Menin declares 'Happy Budget Day' and thanks the finance team, Finance Chair Linda Lee, budget negotiating team, Finance Director Nathan Toth, and her senior staff including Miguelina Camilo, Jonathan Shett, Simone Jones, and others for their work during the budget process.
chapters.title: Speaker Menin thanks finance team and leadership on Budget Day

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Good afternoon or now good evening, everyone.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Happy Budget Day.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: So first of all, I want to thank our incredible finance team who have been here night after night after night.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: For their amazing work during the budget process.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And I particularly want to thank all the council members, particularly our finance chair, Linda Lee.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Members of the budget negotiating team, and council leadership.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I want to thank our finance director, Nathan Toth.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Thank you so much for your incredible work.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I want to thank my whole senior team, Miguelina Camilo, for her amazing work, Jonathan Shett, Simone Jones, Bob Tiberwell, John Tritt,

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Special shout out to Paul Shimon and Olivia DeLuca.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

chapters.summary: Menin highlights that the budget secures the most ever capital and expense funding for the Council. She details the historic CityFEPs agreement with the administration to drop the lawsuit, expand housing vouchers, and create a new cost-containing structure sponsored by Council Member Pierina Sanchez.
chapters.title: Speaker Menin on historic budget investments and CityFEPs settlement

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: This is a budget that secures the most ever capital and expense funding for the City Council in its history, while also demonstrating at the same time prudent fiscal responsibility.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I want to briefly highlight a few transformative investments that we are doing in this budget that meaningfully tackle the affordability crisis.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: With a substantial investment to sensibly expand housing vouchers, we are helping to keep thousands of people in the city.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: more families in their homes and out of shelters.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: In addition to the investment, we reached a truly historic agreement on city FEPS with the administration.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And I just want to take a moment to talk about it.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: They have agreed something we've asked for since January, and I particularly want to thank Councilmember Pierina Sanchez for her leadership on this.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: So we were able to get the administration to drop the lawsuit against the council, which is something that we were insisting on since January, and at the same time

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: engage in a settlement where we will be voting today on a new bill sponsored by Councilmember Sanchez that will create a new cost-containing structure for housing vouchers so that we ensure that we are housing

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: vulnerable New Yorkers, but we're doing it in a way that is truly sustainable.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And that is historic.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: We also held the line on this expansion of the vouchers, and I want to thank all the council members who joined in holding the line on that.

chapters.summary: Menin announces the largest expansion of Fair Fares in council history, extending eligibility to 200% of the federal poverty level for 1.3 million working New Yorkers. She also announces NYC Kids Rise, providing $1,000 college savings accounts for every public school kindergartner, the largest such program in any city.
chapters.title: Speaker Menin on Fair Fares expansion and NYC Kids Rise college savings

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: We also expanded eligibility for fare fares to residents earning up to 200%

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: of the federal poverty level, making public transportation more affordable for nearly 1.3 million working New Yorkers, whether they're taking the subway, the bus, or paratransit.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: This is the largest expansion of fare fares.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: affairs and city council history.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: The council is also enhancing NYC Kids Rise to provide a college savings account with $1,000 for every single public school kindergartner in New York City.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: This is the largest college savings program that any city has ever done.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: It can be used for vocational school.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: It can be used for a two-year community college.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: It can be used for a four-year college.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And it is truly transformative because studies show that low-income children who have a college savings account are three times more likely to go to ongoing education.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And just as importantly, they are likely to earn almost double the wages, double the wages during the course of their lifetime as someone who does not have that college savings account.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: If we truly want to tackle income inequality, this is one of the single best ways we can do so.

chapters.summary: Menin outlines investments in mental health, swim safety, home-delivered meals, children's education, seniors aging in place, libraries at highest ever funding levels, HPD affordable housing on public library sites, $20 million more for cultural institutions, legal and immigrant legal services, $14.4 million for parks, and $5 million for deed theft prevention.
chapters.title: Speaker Menin on budget investments in mental health, seniors, libraries, culture, and legal services

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: We also fought for programs that support mental health, swim safety, home-delivered meals.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: We fought for our children to bolster their opportunities at a good education.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: We fought for our seniors to help them age in place with dignity.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And we fought to fully fund our libraries to the highest ever levels.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I just want to repeat that.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: This budget funds the libraries at our insistence to the highest levels ever.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And it also funds HPD's capital increase to build on that project that we put out a couple of years ago.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: couple weeks ago to build 100% affordable housing on some public libraries throughout the city.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Because our city is also the world's cultural capital, we're investing 20 million more in cultural institutions.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And to provide more people with a path to justice, we fully restored funding

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: for a range of legal service providers, including immigrant legal service providers, which is so critically important, particularly at this time.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: To support the management of clean parks, we are restoring $14.4 million in baseline funding

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And to support black home ownership, we've secured more than $5 million in funding for deed theft prevention programs and legal counseling.

chapters.summary: Menin highlights $350 million additional set aside for the Rainy Day Fund, a record 75 council terms and conditions for transparency, and 11 brand new terms covering child care vouchers, Fair Fares, PACT, OTI contracts, and more. She declares the budget a win for affordability, stability, and opportunity.
chapters.title: Speaker Menin on fiscal responsibility, Rainy Day Fund, and transparency terms

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Even with these life-changing investments, we ensured that our city is on a firm financial footing.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And we're able to push for more funds for the Rainy Day Fund, something that we've been doing since day one of this budget negotiation.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And so I'm thrilled to say that an additional $350 million has been set aside for the Rainy Day Fund, because it is our duty to the people of our city to deliver a balanced and responsible budget

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: not just for next year, but for many years to come.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: To further the spirit of good governance, our adopted budget includes a record 75 council terms and conditions that advance transparency by mandating agency reporting associations.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: With spending.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Of the 11 brand new council terms and conditions, we've covered important topics like child care vouchers, fare fares, PACT, PHPT conversions, OTI citywide contracts, and clubhouses, and many other items.

chapters.summary: Menin wishes happy birthdays to Council Members Linda Lee and Amanda Farias, acknowledges the nation's 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, recounts marching in the Pride March past the Stonewall Inn, recognizes Disability Pride Month, and welcomes Government Advocacy Fellows from the Auschwitz Jewish Center Foundation.
chapters.title: Speaker Menin on birthdays, 250th anniversary, Pride, and welcoming fellows

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: This budget is truly a win for New Yorkers who deserve affordability, stability, and opportunity, and a government devoted to delivering all three.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: So I just want to say congratulations to all the council members, to all our staff at the City Council on this historic win.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: We have a few more things to celebrate.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I want to wish an early happy birthday to Councilmember Linda Lee, whose birthday is on the 3rd of July.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I know she forgot, she's working so hard, she forgot it was her birthday.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And to Councilmember Amanda Fadiaz, whose birthday is on the 9th.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And we have one more birthday to acknowledge.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: This Saturday, oh, Simka's very excited about the birthday.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Okay, thank you, Simka, thank you.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: So we have one more birthday to acknowledge, our nation's birthday.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: This Saturday will mark 250 years since the second Continental Congress officially adopted the Declaration of Independence.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I hope that as we spend a day off with our families, we also think about the meaning of this milestone, and that's up to us to decide where our nation will go from here.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: This past weekend, I was proud to join many council members as we participated in the Pride March.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: We marched right past the Stonewall Inn, where 57 years ago, LGBTQIA plus New Yorkers firmly defended their right to love.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: The council stands with the queer community and we will continue to protect the civil rights of all.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Ahead of tomorrow, July, is Disability Pride Month, where we recognize the countless contributions that disabled residents past and present have made to our city.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: As New Yorkers, we truly know that diversity is our strength, including diversity of ability.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Finally, I'd like to welcome the Government Advocacy Fellows from the Auschwitz Jewish Center Foundation.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I think we're here.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Hello, fellows.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Thank you so much for joining us.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: There's no better way to learn about city government than to see it.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: up close and personal for yourself.

chapters.summary: Menin outlines the agenda: voting on the $125.8 billion FY2027 budget, finance resolutions sponsored by Council Member Lee on expense budget designations, and property tax interest rates at 6%, 9%, 16%, and 2.5% for installment agreements.
chapters.title: Speaker Menin agenda overview - FY2027 budget and finance items

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: So thank you to Councilmember Dinowitz for providing this opportunity.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Now let's move on to our stated agenda.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: First, we're gonna vote on a $125.8 billion budget for fiscal year 2027.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Next, we will vote on the following finance items.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Pre-considered resolutions sponsored by Councilmember Lee, approving the new designation and changes in the designation of certain organizations to receive funding in the expense budget.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Pre-considered resolutions, also sponsored by Councilmember Lee, to establish that the interest rate be 6%

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: per annum for fiscal year 2027 for non-payment of taxes on properties with an assessed value of up to $250,000,

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: 9% for properties with an assessed value of more than $250,000, but less than or equal to $450,000, and 16% for properties with an assessed value of more than $450,000.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: A pre-considered resolution sponsored also by Councilmember Lee to establish that the interest rate be 2.5%

chapters.summary: Menin previews Resolution 69 by Council Member Cabán opposing Trump's executive order restricting protections for transgender students, and Resolution 143-A by Council Member Riley calling for unlimited MTA transfers within two hours for pay-per-ride users.
chapters.title: Speaker Menin agenda overview - Res 69 and Res 143-A

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: per annum for fiscal year 2027 for certain properties for which owner has entered into an installment payment agreement with the Department of Finance for payment of delinquent property taxes, assessments, or other charges.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Today, we will also vote on the following pieces of legislation.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Resolution 69, sponsored by Councilmember Tiffany-Cabán, would oppose President Trump's executive order restricting protections for transgender students.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: and call on the New York State Department of Education to ensure that all schools in New York City

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: continue to uphold policies that protect the rights and dignity of transgender and gender nonconforming students, regardless of federal directives.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Thank you to staff Elizabeth Arts.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Resolution 143-A, sponsored by Councilmember Kevin Riley, would call on the New York State Legislature to pass and the governor to sign legislation that would create unlimited transfers within the two-hour

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: period of paying the MTA subway or bus fare for pay-per-ride users.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Thank you to Sierra Townsend, Theodore Miller, Kevin...

chapters.summary: Menin previews three Knicks resolutions: celebrating Knicks in five (sponsored by Menin), declaring June 13 as Knicks Day (sponsored by Maloney), and ceremonial street namings for Knicks players and coaches across the five boroughs (sponsored by Deputy Speaker Williams).
chapters.title: Speaker Menin agenda overview - Knicks championship resolutions

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Kotowski and John Basile.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: To celebrate the amazing 2026 champion New York Knicks, we are passing three resolutions.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Yay, thank you.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: We've got this side of the room is a much better cheering section than this side of the room, I will just say.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Pre-considered resolution sponsored by myself would celebrate Knicks in five as the New York Knicks won the 2026 NBA championship.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Yay, okay.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
