chapters.summary: Council Member Aldebol discloses NYPD funding association, thanks Mayor Mamdani and Speaker Menin, and praises the budget for addressing food insecurity, older adults, youth, immigrants, schools, libraries, and parks. She notes missing pay parity for EMT workers and DOE paraprofessionals. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Aldebol explains vote - first budget supporting working families

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the New York City Police Department is funded in the budget we are adopting and my business associate is associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: First, I want to thank Mayor Mamdani and Speaker Menon, along with Chair Lee and OMB staff, the City Council Finance Team, for all their hard work in reaching this budget agreement.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: In my first year as a City Council member, I am proud to support a budget that puts the needs of everyday New Yorkers first.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: While there's room for continued progress, this budget is a step forward in our efforts to make New York City more affordable for working families.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: I will always fight for the East Bronx to get our fair share.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: During the budget process, we stood firm with my city council colleagues to ensure we passed a balanced budget without additional tax increases on hardworking Bronx families and homeowners.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: I advocated for funding initiatives to combat food insecurity, support older adults, increase opportunities for young people, and protect our immigrant communities.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: We prioritize funding much-needed capital improvements to our public schools, libraries, and parks.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: In the East Bronx communities I represent, housing and security is a top concern.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: That's why I'm particularly excited that we have secured a historic agreement to expand housing voucher access for New Yorkers facing eviction and homelessness.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: We are also putting additional investment towards supporting homeowners facing rising costs and struggling to keep their homes.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: I must say, however, that a commitment for pay parity for EMT workers and pay increases for DOE paraprofessionals is not apparent in this budget.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: As chair of the Civil Service and Labor Committee, I remain committed to advocate for pay parity improvements for these vital city workers.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: Today, I am proud to vote for a budget that takes strides towards addressing the needs and priorities of hardworking Bronx families and New York City families.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: I look forward to continuing to work with Speaker Numenin, my City Council colleagues, and the administration to build on the strong foundation of this budget in the years to come.

utterances.speaker: Shirley Aldebol
utterances.text: I vote aye on all.

chapters.summary: Council Member Ariola discloses DSNY association, criticizes the mayor for stripping funding for a fifth firefighter, police staffing, and EMS pay while overfunding political projects. She votes no on most budget and finance items, aye on the rest.
chapters.title: Ariola explains vote - voting no on budget over mayor's public safety cuts

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Ariola.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the New York City Department of Sanitation is funded in the budget that we are adopting, and my son is associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: There are a lot of good things in this budget, and it's a shame that this was tainted by the mayor's betrayal and ideological demands.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: Demands which put all New Yorkers in harm's way and will only lead the city down a path to ruin.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: His refusal to fund the fifth firefighter in our engine companies, to adequately man our police force, and to raise the pay of our EMS workers, among so many other things, while overfunding his self-serving, politically motivated

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: agencies and projects is unacceptable and, to be completely honest, downright dangerous.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: Not surprisingly, the mayor put ideology over reality in this budget, and for those reasons...

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: I will vote no on intro 31A, 92A,

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: Preconsidered resos 534 and 535.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: Preconsidered resos 535.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: 39, 540, and M69, preconsidered reso 543,

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: M74, preconsidered reso 544, and M80,

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: pre-considered reso 545 and M81, as well as pre-considered reso 546 and M82, and I on all the rest.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Clerks, you get that?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Yes, sir.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Avilés discloses associations with Uprose, DOE, and Parks. She champions budget justice, praises baselined parks funding, notes NYPD headcount kept flat with investments in mental health teams, and expresses disappointment that Rentals Within Reach and EMT pay parity were not addressed. Votes no on Intro 553A and 929A, aye on rest.
chapters.title: Avilés explains vote - budget justice, parks baselining, NYPD flat

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Aviles.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council that Uprose and the New York City Department of Education and Parks and Recreation are funded in this budget that we are adopting,

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: and I or my child is respectively and sibling are associated with these entities.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: Since being elected to the City Council, I have championed the idea of budget justice, that by turning away from the status quo

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: and austerity budgeting, we can actually fund the services poor and working New Yorkers need by focusing on dismantling economic inequities and shifting funding away from ineffective and harmful programs.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: I've been proud to work with the Progressive Caucus in efforts to center New York's most vulnerable from our crisis to care, public safety priorities, to funding to protect affordable housing and home ownership, to protecting immigrant legal service funding.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: I'm proud to continue supporting local service providers such as Misteca, Center for Family Life, Red Hook Initiative, among so many more.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: Thank you all for the work you do of loving and serving our district every single day.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: It is great to see that the parks funding is baselined among so many other initiatives.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: And I'm taking, I'm, and.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: a taken out of the yearly budget dance.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: We need to get to 1%.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: I'm reassured that this final budget kept the NYPD headcount flat and instead invested more resources in the services that New Yorkers need towards sustainable, data-driven public safety

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: measures, such as mental health treatment teams, including access for EMS workers, and will move New York City closer to our vision of true public safety.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: While I am thrilled that a settlement was reached in CityFEP's case and funding was allocated to expand the program, I am disappointed that the Progressive Caucus'Rentals Within Reach program

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: campaign to reduce existing NYCHA and supportive housing vacancies did not see the meaningful investments it should have, nor have we addressed EMT pay parity.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: Rest assured, we will continue to unapologetically fight for the working people of New York City.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Alexa Avilés
utterances.text: And lastly, I am voting no on Intro 553A and Intro 929A and aye on the rest.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Banks discloses Parks and Juvenile Justice associations. He praises budget investments but calls for more NYCHA funding, an immediate moratorium on new RAD/PACT conversions, resident voting rights on conversions, and prioritizing Section 9 modernization. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Banks explains vote - NYCHA investment, anti-privatization, RAD/PACT concerns

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Banks.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceeding that the Department of Parks and Recreation and the Juvenile Justice are funded in this budget and we are adopting and my brothers are associated with these entities.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: Thank you, Majority Leader.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: Thank you, Madam Speaker, to the leadership team and to the Finance Division.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: Thank you for your tireless work.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: To Mark, my colleagues, thank you for your tremendous work on this year's budget.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: This year budgeted, as stated by the speaker earlier, delivered the largest package of investments secured in the city council history, both capital and expense.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: It makes critical investments in addressing the ongoing affordability crisis here in New York City while expanding opportunity and improving the quality of life of all New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: We expanded mental health services, strengthened affordable and supportive housing, and invested in preparing vacant NYCHA units for occupancy and increased support for older adults across our city.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: And while these investments move our city forward, including steps to bring more NYCHA units back online, we must be honest that they do not yet meet the full scale of the need for facing public housing residents.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: As we recognize this progress, we must also confront

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: where our city is still falling short.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: For me, that begins with public housing.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: For decades, NYCHA has suffered from chronic underinvestment, but let me be clear, the answers to this investment is not privatization.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: The answer is investment.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: Public housing deserves public investment, and public land must remain for public use.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: This is why I will continue calling for an immediate moratorium on new RADPAC conversions until we fully evaluate their long-term impact on residents and their rights.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: And instead, we must prioritize direct investment in traditional Section 9 housing by expanding comprehensive modernization program.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: This is how we will repair and rehabilitate and modernize NYCHA while keeping it permanently public.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: And when it's a community's future is at stake, residents deserve more than a presentation.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: They deserve a voice.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: Every night your resident should have the right in a formal democratic process to vote on whether or not they want to go rad pack.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Please tell us your vote, Councilmember.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: Thank you, Majority Leader.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: I vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Appreciate you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Chris Banks
utterances.text: Save section 8.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: There was no sass in that, I promise.

chapters.summary: Council Member Brewer discloses Hunter College association. She thanks the mayor and Speaker for mental health, housing, CityFEPs, DCWP, seniors, CUNY OmniCards, libraries, and parks funding, and calls for continued attention to oversight agencies like COIB and DOI. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Brewer explains vote - mental health, CUNY, libraries, oversight agencies

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Brewer.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I also teach at Hunter, so I am saying that I am adopting the fact that is true and I am associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I want to say about the budget to thank the mayor and his staff, Speaker Menon and her staff.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I think in every community we have mental health issues that are hopefully being addressed, housing issues, FAPS, NYCHA supportive units, all in the budget to a certain extent.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: And certainly Department of Consumer and Worker Protection has improved its ability hopefully to do outreach, legal services, cultural.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I really want to thank you, Madam Speaker, for the support there.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Seniors need the home delivery.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: CUNY needs the Omni car.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: That's exciting, as well as all the other programs, libraries, and parks.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I also want to thank everyone for helping with the 9-11 to see if there really were toxic discussions that took place.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: We need to know it so that those can hopefully be addressed in the future if there are any such issues.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I finally want to just state that this budget, the only thing missing, we have to keep paying attention, is the oversight agencies, be it

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: COI, be it DOI, which did get more money, or any other agency that does oversight of our governance.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Thank you very much.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I vote aye on all.

chapters.summary: Council Member Brooks-Powers discloses multiple associations. She rises with reservations, noting this is the first administration where the mayor's executive capital budget includes no investments for her district despite health and infrastructure challenges. She praises Council wins including Fair Fares, CityFEPs, $10M for Rockaway trauma center, and $5M for Brookville Park Recreation Center. Votes aye.
chapters.title: Brooks-Powers explains vote - no capital for District 31 under mayor

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Brooks Powers.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: I am disclosing on the record of the council of the council.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: the proceedings that the Queens Alumni Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Incorporated is funded in the budget we are adopting, and I am associated with this nonprofit, and that the New York City Department of Education is funded

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: in the budget we are adopting, and my sibling is associated with this nonprofit.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: Also, Edge School of the Arts is funded in the budget, and we are adopting, and my child is associated with this nonprofit.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: Today I rise in support of this year's budget, but I do so with reservations.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: This is my sixth budget in the City-Council, having served under three mayoral administrations.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: This is the first administration during my tenure where the mayor's executive capital budget

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: includes no capital investments for the 31st Council District at a time when my community faces longstanding health and infrastructure challenges.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: Just four days ago, the mayor said health care is a human right and we will do everything in our power to defend it.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: But those words ring hollow for my community today.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: The absence of capital funding in this budget for the 31st District sends a clear message that your zip code does determine your right to health care.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: A budget reflects our values and every community deserves to see itself in that vision.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: In the weeks ahead, the mayor must...

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: Affirm his commitment to District 31 by sharing his vision for our community.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: I will not stop fighting to ensure our district is not left behind under this administration.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: That being said, I want to thank Speaker Menon for her leadership in negotiating a budget that reflects many of the Council's priorities from raising fair fares eligibility

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: to 200% of the federal poverty level to the expansion of City FEPS to a historic investment in affordable housing preservation and new home ownership construction.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: This budget brings us closer to delivering a trauma center to the Rockaway Peninsula by including $10 million for this critical project thanks to this council.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: This budget also provides an additional $5 million for our long-awaited Brookville Park Recreation Center that will create new opportunities for families and young people throughout Southeast Queens.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: I am supporting this budget because of what the council fought to deliver for working families across New York City, but much more work lies ahead.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: Thank you, and I vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Come on.

chapters.summary: Council Member Cabán thanks finance staff and Progressive Caucus. She highlights $26 million for NYC Against Hate, $80 million baselined for mental health, DCWP funding for worker rights, and no NYPD headcount increase. She notes shortcomings on Rentals Within Reach and FEPS expansion. Votes aye on budget, no on Intro 553A and 929A, aye on rest.
chapters.title: Cabán explains vote - progressive wins on mental health, hate prevention, FEPS

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: Thank you, colleagues, Speaker, Finance Chair, and to the Council Finance staff for your tireless work on this budget.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: When first elected to the City Council, I made a promise to my constituents and to myself to always fight for the most vulnerable New Yorkers, no matter what.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: I believe then, as I do now, that we can build a city where parents can raise families, where people can grow old, where we can remain in our communities free from the fear of displacement, and where our government works for all of us.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: But for too long, municipal budgets have prioritized the interests of the wealthy and well-connected.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: There are elements of this budget that, like those previous budgets, fall short of what working-class New Yorkers need.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: For example, it fails to fund rentals within reach, which would have provided the housing of 20,000 of the most vulnerable New Yorkers through investments in NYCHA and supportive housing.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: And I will say, despite these shortcomings, this budget makes crucial changes that will improve the lives of people across the city.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: When corporations commit wage theft or impose extortionate fees on New Yorkers, DCWP rarely has the resources to hold them accountable.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: And this budget increases their funding to ensure that they can enforce worker and consumer rights laws.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: When New Yorkers fear being attacked or discriminated against because of their identity, they too often are directed to a reactive police response that does little to prevent or help heal.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: Today, we're investing $26 million in NYC Against Hate, which will promote...

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: proven community-based hate violence prevention.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: Too many New Yorkers struggling with mental illness have been left to fend for themselves, or worse, criminalized for it.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: We worked with advocates to baseline over $80 million for mental health programs to deliver care.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: Every day, working class New Yorkers face eviction.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: I'm proud of the Progressive Caucus, the Speaker, the Mayor, to reach a deal to expand FEPS, but it does fall short.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: We can do more.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: And I'm proud to have worked closely with the mayor and public safety advocates to ensure there was no increase to the NYPD's headcount.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: I've said it many times before, and I'll say it again.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: We cannot police, punish, and incarcerate our way to safety.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: And every dollar we spend there is money we can't spend on housing, mental health care, substance abuse, and so on.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: Just as I promised when I first entered the council, we'll continue to fight for the New York we all deserve.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: But I will say for these reasons, I vote aye on the budget, no on Intro 553A and Intro 929A, and aye on all the rest.

utterances.speaker: Tiffany L. Cabán
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member De La Rosa discloses DOE association. She thanks Mayor Mamdani, Speaker Menin, and Progressive Caucus. She highlights wins on supportive housing, Fair Fares, libraries, immigrant legal services, parks, and CityFEPs, while noting more work on NYCHA vacancies and deed theft. Votes aye on all except no on Intro 929A and 92A.
chapters.title: De La Rosa explains vote - affordable housing, immigrant services, progressive taxation

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: De La Rosa.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: I am disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the New York City Department of Education is funded in this budget we are adopting and my child is associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: I request permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: Thank you, Majority Leader.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: I want to start by thanking Mayor Mamdani and his team, Speaker Menon and her team, Chair Lee, the BNT, and the staff for their work over the last several months.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: I want to thank my colleagues, particularly those in the Progressive Caucus, as well as

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: the coalitions and community advocates who fought tirelessly to protect critical services.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: We worked for a budget that prioritizes affordable housing, social justice amid a deep affordability crisis.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: This budget includes important wins for supportive housing, fair fares, libraries, immigrant legal services,

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: Parks, city effects, crisis to care.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: Still, more work lies ahead to close the urgent housing gaps, including NYCHA vacancy repairs and stronger investments to address deed theft.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: We all share responsibility for one another, but I especially want to commend Councilmember Pierina-Ana-Sanchez for her tireless advocacy on city FEPS and for reminding us all of a collective duty to care for our fellow New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: Our communities are confronting rising costs, growing mental health needs, fear and uncertainty in immigrant communities, and an escalating consequences of climate change.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: Meeting these challenges will require truly progressive taxation that is equitable without reservation.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: New Yorkers have made themselves clear.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: We need an affordable city.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: While the rent freeze on one- and two-year leases offers some relief, our work is far from over.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: I am proud to vote yes on the budget today with a continued commitment to uplift the voices of District 10 and to fight for a city that is rooted in affordability, safety, and dignity for all.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: With that being said, I want to vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: Except for intro 929A and intro 92A, for which I vote no.

utterances.speaker: Carmen N. De La Rosa
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Dinowitz discloses CUNY association. He reflects on helping constituents at their worst moments and highlights budget deliveries: Fair Fares expansion, CityFEPs, NYC Kids Rise, restorative justice, mental health support, CUNY OmniCards, early childhood seats, and baselined parks funding. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Dinowitz explains vote - delivering for working families on transit, housing, education

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Dinowitz.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that City University of New York is funded in the budget we are adopting, and I am associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: Thank you so much, Majority Leader.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: Good evening.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Good evening.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: Thank you, sir.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: I have to, well, may I rise to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: It's my time.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Your time has started.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: I'll do what I want with that time.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: I'm okay.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: I'm okay.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: No, it's not just this why I love my job.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: I think our job is very special.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: Like, every day we get to help people.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: And the people who come into our office are very often coming in at their worst, right?

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: People who are at their wits end struggling just to get to their job on the bus, people on the verge

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: of eviction, and parents who are anxious and desperate about the success of their children and families who want their parks to be safe and clean.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: And for those families, for those neighbors of ours, we demand the best from our government.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: And today, we are delivering the best in our government.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: So for those workers that

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: come into our office just trying to get to their job and do better for the city, we hear you.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: And we're delivering an expansion of fair fares.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: For the families on the brink of eviction, we hear you.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: And we are delivering expanded city FEPS to keep you housed.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: And for those parents who simply want their children to do better than they were ever able to do, we hear you.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: And we are delivering New York City Kids Rise, $1,000 for every public school, kindergarten investments in restorative justice, mental health support, student success centers, OmniCards for CUNY students, early childhood seats, teacher training, and so much more.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: And for those families who want those safe, clean parks, increased and baseline parks funding.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: The delivery of these services in this budget is due in large part all of us working together under the leadership of Speaker Menning.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: And I just want to thank you, Speaker Menning, for standing firm and delivering a budget that delivers for our constituents, to our finance chair, and to the entire finance staff.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Eric Dinowitz
utterances.text: I vote aye on this budget and on all the legislation.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Encarnación discloses Community Voices Heard association. She celebrates her first budget as a council member and leadership team member, highlighting CityFEPs, Fair Fares expansion, and immigration legal services. She thanks Speaker Menin, Finance Chair Lee, and the finance division. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Encarnación explains vote - first budget, immigration services, CityFEPs

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Encarnación.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that Community Voices Heard is funded in the budget we are adopting and I'm associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: I'd also like permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: Thank you, Majority Leader.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: I am proud to vote in support of this budget, my very first as a member of the City Council and my first as a member of the Council's leadership team.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: It has been an incredible learning experience and I am grateful to everyone whose hard work made this budget possible.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: This budget reflects our shared values from our solidarity behind city FEPS to the incredible expansion of fair fares.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: And as chair of immigration committee, I am proud that we are reaffirming our commitment to immigration legal services, ensuring that immigrant New Yorkers continue to have access to the legal representation and support they deserve.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: These are meaningful investments that will make a real difference in people's lives, and they are the result of collaboration, thoughtful negotiations, and a commitment to delivering for New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: I want to sincerely thank Speaker Menon for her steadfast leadership and for holding the line on one of the council's most important priorities.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: I also want to thank Finance Chair Lee for her partnership and dedication throughout this process, the incredible finance division for their tireless work, and every member of the budget negotiation team and the finance committee.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: It has truly been an honor to be a part of this process, and I am incredibly proud of what we have accomplished.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: With that said, I vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Elsie Encarnación
utterances.text: Thank you, Majority Leader.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: Epstein.

chapters.summary: Council Member Epstein votes aye on all but takes a moment to honor Abdul Saleh, a bodega owner in his district shot and killed two months ago. He calls for continued work on gun violence solutions across the council.
chapters.title: Epstein explains vote - Abdul Saleh street co-naming and gun violence

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: I just want to just, I'm going to vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: I just wanted to talk about one street co-naming.

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: Abdul Saleh was in my neighborhood and about two months ago was shot and killed in a gun violence.

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: Ran a store, a bodega in my district, and it was such a really heartbreaking incident.

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: And, you know, we've talked about gun violence a lot in this chamber and focusing on concerns around that, and we're doing a street co-naming in his behalf.

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: But it goes longer than that, and we have a huge problem in our city and in my district around gun violence.

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: And so hopefully with this street co-naming and the work that I know that we're doing across this council, we can get in front of all these issues.

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: And on behalf of Abdul Saleh and his family and all the other victims of gun violence, I encourage us to continue to work for longer-term solutions.

utterances.speaker: Harvey D. Epstein
utterances.text: I vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Farias thanks the finance team, Speaker Menin, Chair Lee, and Mayor Mamdani for a collaborative budget process. She votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Farias explains vote - historic budget collaboration with mayor

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: Farias.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: There's a new majority leader in this city.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: I actually looked up and was confused for one second.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Well, thank you, interim majority leader Krishnan.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: Sounds nice.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: We're proud to have reached an on-time and historic budget agreement that delivers meaningful investments for all New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: A huge thank you to our incredible finance team for their tireless work, to Speaker Menon, Chair Lee, and obviously across City Hall, Mayor Mamdani and his team for partnering with the council to get this across the finish line.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: We really had a great collaboration this year, and our communities will be better because of it.

utterances.speaker: Amanda C. Farías
utterances.text: And with that, I vote aye and all.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Simcha Felder
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that Turo College, the Stroke and Brain Injury Assistance Organization, and Yellow to Yellow, the

utterances.speaker: Simcha Felder
utterances.text: childhood center funded in the budget that we are adopting, and I, my spouse, and a sibling, respectively, are associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Simcha Felder
utterances.text: I also want to vote no on Reso 530, 531, 532, 533.

utterances.speaker: Simcha Felder
utterances.text: It's in order, so it should be easier.

utterances.speaker: Simcha Felder
utterances.text: And I abstain on Intro 956.

utterances.speaker: Simcha Felder
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilman.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: You know, you're right.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Feliz.

chapters.summary: Council Member Feliz discloses CUNY and Volunteers of America associations. He celebrates CityFEPs expansion, Fair Fares, libraries, parks, and NYC Kids Rise. He highlights his Intro 553-A providing small businesses with security technology including cameras and plexiglass. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Feliz explains vote - Intro 553 small business security, CityFEPs, Fair Fares

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: I am disclosing on the record that CUNY is funded in the budget we're adopting.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: I serve proudly as an adjunct lecturer in the CUNY system.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Also, my parent is associated with Volunteers of America, which is also being funded in this budget.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: With that said, permission takes my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: And I see everybody standing up for their statements, so I'll do the same.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: I want to start by congratulating our wonderful speaker, Julie-Menin, also our finance chair, Linda-Lee, and all of my colleagues on fighting for a budget that helps resolve the issues of our time,

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: including the issue of housing affordability, expanding the CityFAPS program so that rather than living in a temporary shelter, our most vulnerable families can live in a stable home with the dignity that they deserve.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Also, public transit affordability, expanding the fare-fares program so that costs related to travel do not become an obstacle when residents are in search of opportunity.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Housing affordability, public transit affordability, and so much more.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: We fought to properly fund our libraries, our parks.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Please keep it down in the chambers.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Our kids'rights program.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: to ensure that we're truly preparing our youth for the future.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: So many big wins for the hardworking people of this city.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: This is a budget that we should all be proud of.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: I'm also proud and thankful that we're finally voting to approve Intro 553, the Small Business Protection Program.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Or Small Business Protection Act.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Small businesses are the heart of our economy.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: They create local jobs, they make communities vibrant.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: They do so much and it is crucial that we listen to them and help them resolve the challenges that they face, including challenges related to safety.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Some of our businesses continue to face challenges related to safety, problems that are affecting their ability to stay afloat.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: My bill will ensure that businesses have the tools to help prevent safety issues by equipping them with technology, including cameras, plexiglass for the bodegas, and more to help deter.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: This is an important step that we're taking to protect small business owners and their hard work.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: We're also sending a very crystal clear message.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Now when it comes to the safety issues that they've consistently raised, this City Council is listening.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: So with that said, I want to thank you, Speaker Manon, for helping get that bill to the finish line.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: I want to congratulate everybody.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: I vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Oswald J. Feliz
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Perfect timing.

chapters.summary: Council Member Gennaro discloses Queens Centers for Progress association. He highlights his new Heat Pumps for All initiative providing cost-free heat pumps to low-income homeowners in environmental justice communities, supported by labor and environmental advocates. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Gennaro explains vote - Heat Pumps for All pilot program

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Gennaro.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: I am disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the Queens Centers for Progress is funded in the budget and the budget we are adopting and my spouse is associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: My spouse is Wendy and she's watching.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: And so there you go.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: I request permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: I am really delighted that this budget includes a new initiative, Heat Pumps for All, a pilot program of cost-free heat pumps for low-income one- and two-family homeowners in environmental justice communities.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: I am proud to have introduced this initiative in this year's...

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: Budget process with support from Finance Chair Linda Lee, Councilmember Aviles, Councilmember Nurse, Councilmember Marte, the BNT, and of course, Speaker Menon and all of her terrific staff.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: Special shout out to Nason Toth.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: We have created a heat pumps coalition, including the Central Labor Council in New York.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: New York Communities for Change, Steamfitters Local 3638, Climate Jobs New York, Food and Water Watch, and NYPERG.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: It was great working side by side with environmental advocates and labor in common cause to create a city with clean air and clean energy union jobs.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: This is the formula for a more sustainable city.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: This pilot will help.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: help to normalize the heat pump technology that we badly need to meet our clean air and climate challenges, and will generate data on the efficacy of this technology.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: I again want to thank Speaker Menon for her great environmental leadership to advance this effort and for recognizing the importance of expanding access to clean, affordable,

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: heating and cooling for working families and the creation of green union jobs.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: This funding is an important first step towards ensuring that working families have the same opportunity to modernize their homes, lower their energy bills, and contribute to a cleaner, more resilient New York City.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: And last but not least, I thank my terrific legislative director sitting right behind me, Josh.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: Gachet.

utterances.speaker: James F. Gennaro
utterances.text: With that said, I vote aye on all.

chapters.summary: Council Member Gutierrez discloses multiple associations. She thanks the Progressive Caucus leadership, highlights $2.5 million for CUNY early child care workforce development, and celebrates CityFEPs expansion. Votes aye except no on Intro 929, abstain on Intro 553.
chapters.title: Gutierrez explains vote - CUNY child care workforce, CityFEPs, Progressive Caucus

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I am disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the New York City Department of Social Services and Education and my Monadnids Medical Center are funded in the budget we are adopting and my spouse, child, and sibling respectively are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I want to first start off by giving all the thank yous.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I want to double down on thanking the entire finance staff, Speaker Menon, the Mamdani administration, all the staff.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: This has been quite the experience, and I'm really excited that in a few hours it will be behind us.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I want to give a special shout out to the Progressive Caucus, to our champions, the Progressive Caucus leadership, Council Members Nurse, Kavan, Aviles, Santosuoso,

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: NPC member, Pirina Sanchez, that have been incredible partners in not just getting these critical, critical investments

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: to our budget, but really helping to prioritize all the needs for working class New Yorkers, for the most vulnerable New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I am really excited that the administration is funding the early child care workforce development at CUNY.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: We had a hearing a couple months ago now, and while I am obviously in support of the expansion and the rollout of all things universal child care, I've been concerned about our ability to

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: match the number of seats and expansion with workforce, aka filling the classrooms with educators to do that work.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: CUNY has been doing this work for a very long time.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: They are up for the challenge to expand, and so I am super, super excited to see the 2.5 million investment to support this growing workforce so that anybody who's looking to

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: pursue a profession in early child care education can be trained and prepared to take this on.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I, of course, am very grateful that we were able to secure the funding for expanding city FEPS.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: You've heard from so many of my colleagues, you've heard from Speaker Menon, just how important it has been to invest in expanding this voucher because

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: creating this program allows every New Yorker who is facing eviction or homelessness an opportunity and option.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: And for many of us here, before we were members, we were one paycheck away from that as well.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: So it's, oh, that's it?

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: That's my whole time?

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Oh my God.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: I vote aye, except for intro 929, and I abstain from intro 553.

utterances.speaker: Jennifer Gutiérrez
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Hanif praises Mayor Mamdani's budget as not one of austerity, highlighting baselined Fair Fares, mental health responders, Promise NYC childcare, no NYPD headcount increase, and CityFEPs. She calls for taxing the rich and continuing to fight for NYCHA and disability funding. Votes aye on all except no on 929.
chapters.title: Hanif explains vote - democratic socialist priorities, tax the rich

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Hanif.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: I vote yes on the budget.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: Thanks to the leadership of Democratic Socialist Mayor Mamdani, the budget before us is not a budget of austerity.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: It is a budget that shows what is possible when we choose to invest in poor and working people and care.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: Fair Fares is baselined and expanded so more New Yorkers can get around our city.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: Longtime priorities of the Progressive Caucus, mental health responders, crisis respite centers, and initiatives that hire peer specialist staff are baselined.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: Promise NYC, which provides free childcare for newly arrived immigrant families, is baselined.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: And importantly, there is no increase in NYPD headcount, an issue I strongly advocated for during negotiations.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: This council also secured a historic agreement on city FEPS.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: At a time when too many families remain trapped in shelter, this investment creates a pathway to permanent housing, stability, community, and opportunity.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: These victories are significant, but our work is not done.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: I look forward to continuing the fight to fully invest in NYCHA so vacant apartments can be repaired and returned to families faster.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: I will also continue advocating for full funding of the Mayor's Office for People with Disabilities, the disability justice arm of our city government.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: And let me be clear, New Yorkers deserve even more.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: We must tax the rich.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: Alongside the Progressive Caucus and my socialist colleagues, I will continue fighting every year for the resources we need to deliver housing, transit, schools, healthcare, and public services that every New Yorker deserves.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: Thank you, Speaker Menon and the budget negotiating team.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: Today, I am proud to be a democratic socialist.

utterances.speaker: Shahana K. Hanif
utterances.text: I vote aye on all except for 929.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Hankerson briefly thanks Speaker Menin for parks advocacy and capital funding for Baisley Pond Park, and the mayor for baselining street sweeping on Liberty Avenue and forgiving Rochdale Village water bill interest. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Hankerson explains vote - parks, Baisley Pond Park, street sweeping

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Hankerson.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: I'll be very brief because I spoke already, but I just want to thank Speaker Menon again for her advocacy on parks as well as the entire finance team, Nathan and his team, for the hard work that they did over these past few months.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: I want to thank the Speaker's Office for the capital funding for Baisley Pond Park.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: It is something that my constituents have been waiting and looking for, and so thank you for that.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: I also want to thank the mayor and his team for baselining street sweeping funding for the first time ever for Liberty Avenue, as well as negotiating a deal for the good folks at Rochdale Village to forgive their interest on their water bill.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Ty Hankerson
utterances.text: I vote aye or no.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Hanks praises the budget for making life more affordable without raising property taxes or cutting services. She highlights NYC Kids Rise, Fair Fares, cultural institutions, and advocates for first responders and public safety. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Hanks explains vote - NYC Kids Rise, public safety, no property tax increases

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Hanks.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: Today I'm proud to vote yes on the FY27 budget.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: It delivers real investments for you New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: This is a budget that makes

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: life more affordable, strengthens neighborhoods, and protects essential services.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: I want to thank Speaker Menon, Finance Chair Linda Lee, and the Council's finance staff and everyone who worked through this process to deliver a responsible budget that meets this moment.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: This budget makes meaningful investments in fair fares, homeowners, older adults,

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: cultural institution, and it also proves an important point.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: We can invest our communities without raising property taxes, raiding reserves, or cutting essential services.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: I'm especially proud of the expansion of NYC Kids Rise.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: Every New York City public school kindergartner will now have a stronger financial foundation from the very beginning, and that is how we help families build wealth, expand opportunity, and invest in the next generation.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: But I also want to be clear, throughout this process, we fought for our first responders.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: We fought for the additional resources for our police officers and firefighters to keep New Yorkers safe.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: Public safety must always remain a priority, and we will continue that fight.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: As a member of this body and a representative of District 49, my commitment remains the same, to support responsible investments that strengthen our neighborhoods, improve lives, and deliver for the people we serve.

utterances.speaker: Kamillah Hanks
utterances.text: Speaker Menon, you made us proud with a budget that reflects our values, and I am proud to support a budget that meets this moment for New Yorkers, and I vote aye on all.

chapters.summary: Council Member Hudson discloses multiple cultural institution associations. She highlights $78 million for Fair Fares, SNAP educators, baselined Crisis to Care mental health programs, CityFEPs expansion, and deed theft funding. She abstains on Intro 929A, votes aye on the rest.
chapters.title: Hudson explains vote - SNAP, Fair Fares, CityFEPs, Rentals Within Reach

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Hudson.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings at the Brooklyn Museum, Botanical Garden, Academy of Music, Museum of the City of New York, New York City.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: Commission on Gender Equity and Unlocal are funded in the budget we are adopting, and I, my spouse, and my business associate, respectively, are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: One of the most challenging but crucial roles of this body is our charter mandate to approve the city's budget.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: And while no budget is perfect and there is never enough money to fully fund every item, there's always something to be grateful for.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: And this budget includes meaningful wins.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: It properly budgets HRA programs like cash assistance, adds SNAP eligibility specialists, invests $78 million to expand fair fares to 200% of the federal poverty line,

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: and for the first time funds SNAP educators to help New Yorkers access benefits after the Trump administration eliminated federal support for this work.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: And the Progressive Caucus's fiscal year 26 campaign, Crisis to Care, is now baselined, while this budget secures tens of millions of dollars to fill supportive housing and NYCHA vacancies as demanded by the Progressive Caucus's fiscal year 27 rentals within REACH campaign.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: I also want to call out this budget's commitment to city FEPS, $175 million in additional funds to stabilize the program and get us closer toward the expansion

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: this council has fought for since 2023, which would not have happened without the leadership of the Progressive Caucus and tireless advocacy of social service providers and impacted New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: We will not stop fighting for additional funding to implement these laws, and I'm committed to continuing my advocacy for budget priorities that meet the urgency this moment demands.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: including allowing people to remain in their homes by funding the tools they need to combat deed theft.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: Lastly, I want to thank council finance staff as well as those at OMB for getting us to this point, quite literally.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: I want to especially thank Julia Haramis, Elizabeth Childers-Garcia, and Farah Rahman from the general welfare team.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: And thank you to the speaker and mayor for their leadership.

utterances.speaker: Crystal Hudson
utterances.text: I vote aye with the exception of intro 929A, for which I abstain.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Joseph discloses DOE and nonprofit associations. As Higher Education Chair, she highlights $700,000 for CUNY OmniCards, CUNY Reconnect, CUNY ACE, ASAP, disability services, and early childhood workforce development. She also celebrates District 40 investments. Votes aye.
chapters.title: Joseph explains vote - historic CUNY investments, OmniCards, District 40

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Joseph.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceeding that my child is associated with New York City Public Schools that we are funding in this budget.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: And also my child is associated with ROSIS and Moving Mountains.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: Thank you, Majority Leader.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: Today we're finalizing new investments in people of New York City, which reflect our commitment to opportunity, safety, economic mobility, and stronger communities.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: As the chair of Committee on Higher Education, I'm especially proud that this budget delivers historic investments in CUNY.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: Because when we invest in CUNY, we invest in New York City's future.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: CUNY is the economic engine of our city.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: It is where first-generation students, working parents, immigrants, veterans, and lifelong learners gain the education and skills that strengthen our workforce, our neighborhood.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: It is one of the greatest pathways to middle class, and this budget recognizes that.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: This year, we secured $700,000 for a pilot program providing free OmniCards for CUNY students, helping remove one of the biggest barriers to college success, transportation.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: No student should have to choose between getting to class and paying for groceries or rent.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: We are investing in the programs that we know change lives, expanding support for CUNY Reconnect, strengthening CUNY ACE, CUNY ASAP, enhancing CUNY disability services,

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: and growing early childhood workforce development at CUNY to build the next generation of educators our city urgently need.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: As the chair of the Higher Education Committee,

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: I'm also equally proud that this budget continues to invest in our youngest learners, strengthening our public schools, and support the educators and families who make our education system work every day.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: I'm also thrilled that we're bringing home critical investments for residents of District 40.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: This budget delivers meaningful resources for our neighborhoods, schools, our parks, our cultural institutions, and community organizations that make Central Brooklyn such a vibrant place to live.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: These investments will improve the quality of life, expand opportunities for our young people, and strengthen the neighborhood we proudly call home.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: Today we're also here more, there's more work ahead.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: However, this budget is an example when we accomplish, when we work together and put New Yorkers first.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: I want to thank the mayor, the speaker, and all of the staff and countless students, educators, labor partners, and community organizations for their partnership in delivering.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: These important investments.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: Together we have produced a budget that invests in our students, supports working families, strengthening public safety and advances our neighborhoods, and build a more equitable future for all New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Rita C. Joseph
utterances.text: And I vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Krishnan discloses multiple associations. He highlights parks worker protections, swim safety initiatives, DOI funding increase, $4 million for 9/11 air quality investigation, $2.1 million for non-traditional worker organizing, and CityFEPs. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Krishnan explains vote - parks, workers' rights, 9/11 air quality investigation

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Krishnan.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: I am disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the New York City Department of Education, Common Point, New York City, the YMCA, and CUNY Queens College are funded in the budget we are adopting, and my children are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: Our city council, under the leadership of Speaker Menon and Finance Chair Lee, have secured the largest package of capital and expense investments secured by any city council in New York City history.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: I'm thankful for the leadership of our council and Mayor Mamdani for delivering a

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: Budget for working families.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: This budget fights for all of us.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: It funds our parks, turns the pages on former Mayor Adams'corruption, and strengthens the council's work to protect and support workers.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: We've delivered a massive victory for our parks department.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: The budget we are signing into law makes sure that parks workers don't get handed a pink slip on July 1st, now or ever.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: We've created the first ever swim safety initiatives, giving us the staff, resources, and educational materials to prevent drownings.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: And we also expanded Wave Makers, the program that Speaker and I worked on several years ago that gives second graders free swim lessons across the city.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: For the first time in years, the Department of Investigation saw an increase in their funding that goes beyond the Adams-Yerks cuts and slashes.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: That means we can hold bad landlords, big corporations, and government agencies who are letting us down accountable.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: And with the $4 million increase in funding, with the work of our speaker, Councilmember Brewer and I, we will be able to give the brave men and women who risked their lives during and after 9-11 answers on what our city knew about the air quality.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: This budget is also a workers'rights budget.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: I'm proud that we were able to deliver $2.1 million in funding for the first ever non-traditional worker organizing and education initiative.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: This initiative carries on the historic work we've done in the council to support workers'rights, from decriminalizing street venting to passing the largest workers'rights legislation for Uber and Lyft drivers, putting an end to their unfair firings and giving them notice and due process.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: We'll make sure workers understand their rights.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: and know our city has their back.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: Today, we are voting on a budget that moves us beyond corruption and the prioritization of billionaires to a budget that supports our families, parks, and workers.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: A budget that funds city FEPS to keep families in their homes and gives their kids a chance to go to college for New York City Kids Rise.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: I am proud to vote aye today on a budget that delivers for working families in Queens and across New York City and to vote aye on all the rest too.

utterances.speaker: Shekar Krishnan
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Finance Chair Linda Lee discloses DOE and Korean Community Services associations. She thanks Speaker Menin, advocates, and reads the full list of finance division staff including Nathan Toth, Jonathan Rosenberg, deputy directors, economists, counsel, and analysts. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Lee explains vote - thanks finance team by name

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Lee.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So I'm disclosing for the record of the council proceeding that the New York City Department of Education is funded in the budget we are adopting, and my children are associated with that entity, and that Korean Community Services is funded in this budget we are adopting, and my husband is associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Permission to exclaim my vote?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So I just want to say thank you so much to our speaker for fighting so hard and negotiating such a strong budget that includes so many wins that are historic, many of which my colleagues have already highlighted around CityFEP.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Special shout out to Councilmember Pierina Sanchez, as well as a lot of our colleagues who fought so hard to get that into the budget and negotiate it.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: We still have a lot more work to do, but very thankful for homebound meals for our seniors, as well as a lot of the mental health programming that we know is important and keeps New Yorkers safe.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: But I just really wanted to spend most of my time, well, before I get to that, and special shout out to

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: to all the advocates and providers who participated in all the roundtables, the rallies, the conversations with all of us members to really fight for and advocate a lot of their asks and needs and to guide us in that process.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And special shout out to Councilmember Pierina Sanchez, as well as a lot of our colleagues who fought so hard to get that into the budget and negotiate it.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: We still have a lot more work to do, but very thankful for homebound meals for our seniors, as well as a lot of the mental health programming that we know is important and keeps New Yorkers safe.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: But I wanted to really dedicate my time to recognizing some key people from our finance team that really did such an amazing, amazing job negotiating this budget and putting everything together.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: But I just really wanted to spend most of my time, well, before I get to that, and special shout out to

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
