chapters.summary: Council Member Louis discloses Parks and MTA associations. She celebrates permanent baselining of parks, cultural institutions, and CUNY funding, the homeowner help desk, and CityFEPs expansion. She thanks Speaker Menin, Chair Lee, and the finance team. Votes aye.
chapters.title: Louis explains vote - parks, libraries, CUNY baselining, homeowner help

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Lewis.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I'm disclosing for the record of the City Council proceeding that New York City Parks Department and the MTA are funded in the budget.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: We are adopting and myself and my sibling are respectfully associated with these entities.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Today I'm proud to vote yes on this historic budget.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: This is a fiscally responsible budget that invests in essential services, advances our affordability agenda, and strengthens our city's fiscal future.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: And it does all that.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: All of that without cutting the services our communities depend on.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: For far too long, libraries, parks, cultural organizations, and CUNY have fought every single budget cycle just to keep their doors open.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: This budget changes that, permanently baselining funding for our parks, cultural institutions, and CUNY.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: For homeowners, this budget invests in the homeowner help desk, providing technical support, financial and legal counseling to keep families in their homes.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: And for our neighbors struggling to make the rent, this budget gives crucial investments and expands city FEPS.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: I want to commend Speaker Menon and Chair Lee for being historic history makers with this budget.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Councilmember P.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Sanchez for her advocacy and hard work.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: The entire finance team for taking each and every one of my annoying phone calls.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: And for the speaker staff for your leadership and hard work.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: work and with all that I vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Farah N. Louis
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Maloney discloses multiple associations. She highlights $25 million for Bellevue psychiatric care, library renovations, ADA upgrades at the Met and CUNY Graduate Center, Stuy Town Tenants Association funding, and $300 million added to the Rainy Day Fund. Votes yes.
chapters.title: Maloney explains vote - Bellevue psychiatric care, Rainy Day Fund, Stuy Town

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Maloney.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: First, I want to disclose on the record of the council proceedings that the New York Foundation for Senior Citizens is funded in this budget that we're adopting, and my sister serves on the board.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: And I also want to disclose that City University of New York, the Floating Hospital, 92NY, the Metropolitan Opera Association are funded in the budget, and my mother is associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: A budget is a reflection of values, and this one makes a long-term commitment to our parks, to our libraries, to our cultural institutions by restoring and baselining funding for these services that New Yorkers rely on every day.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: It creates more opportunities for the next generation by increasing New York City

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: kids'rise, college savings accounts, expanding housing vouchers to help more New Yorkers find stable and affordable homes, and I'm proud to share that we have a $25 million

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: commitment to expand psychiatric care at Bellevue Hospital, located in my district.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: This delivers library renovations for Hunter College, ADA upgrades at the Met and CUNY Graduate Center,

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: and funding for Stuy Town Tenants Association, all in my district, the nation's largest tenants association dedicated to preserving affordability.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: And we did all of this while strengthening our city's fiscal footing, adding $300 million to the Rainy Day Fund, bringing that to $400 million this year.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: So congratulations to Speaker Menon, to Finance Chair Lee, to Mayor Mondami, and to all of my colleagues in the New York City Council

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: for delivering a budget that protects essential services, strengthens our neighborhoods, and invests in New York families.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: For all these reasons, I'm proud to vote yes.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Marte discloses multiple associations. He calls for fighting privatization of public housing and ensuring workers are paid. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Marte explains vote - housing, workers, anti-privatization

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Marte.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: I am disclosing on the record of the council's proceedings that Asphalt Green, Fortune Society, and Combody Productions are funded in a budget we are adopting, and I and my sibling are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: Permission to explain my note.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: Initially, I was going to vote no on this budget, but with the work and the commitment that the speaker has done for workers and working class families across our city,

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: and because of chair of our housing committee, Perina Sanchez, and her dedication on holding the line, it's gotten to a point where, you know, this is not.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: All that we wanted, but we got to a place where we have the commitments, we deliver on some, and we have to keep on fighting.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: And some of those fights are, you know, fighting against privatization of public housing, making sure that workers actually get paid for the hours that they work, making sure that people don't have to

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: give their lives just to have a roof above their heads, and that we choose keeping people in their homes instead of putting them in shelter and putting them in a path of

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: horrible outcomes.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: And so I want to thank this council.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: There's a lot more work that needs to be done.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: There's a lot more commitments that need to be made.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: And I look forward in the upcoming weeks that we can deliver, especially for workers in this city who continue to be blindsided by the administration.

utterances.speaker: Christopher Marte
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: I vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilman.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Morano discloses multiple associations. He praises the Speaker and progressive colleagues for united negotiations but votes no on the expense budget, condemning the last-minute stripping of 580 NYPD officers as dishonest governance by the mayor. Votes aye on capital and some items, no on expense budget items.
chapters.title: Morano explains vote - voting no on expense budget over stripped NYPD funding

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: More on all.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the New York City Department of Education, Lose Helping Hand, Victory for the Voiceless, Prospect Heights Open Street Inc., Columbia University, City University of New York,

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: YMCA, and the Italian Club of Staten Island are funded in the budget.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: We're adopting, and my child, spouse, siblings, and I, respectively, are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission, Granson.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: First, I want to express my profound gratitude to our speaker, our finance chair, and the entire budget negotiating team.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Because of their fierce advocacy, this budget delivers incredible, historic capital investments for Staten Island, investments that will benefit our borough for generations.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: This team stood tall, and it's precisely because of their exceptional leadership that I'm enthusiastically voting yes on the capital budget today.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: I also want to praise my progressive colleagues in this chamber.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: We don't always see eye to eye, but during these high-stakes negotiations, nobody blinked.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: My colleagues refused the temptation to be divided or picked off by the other side of City Hall.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: They stood united as honest brokers for what they thought was right, and that's true public service, and I respect all of them for it.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Unfortunately, I can't say the same for the executive branch, and that's why I must vote no on the expense budget.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Yesterday, on the very eve of this vote, we learned that funding for 580 additional NYP

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: officers was quietly stripped.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Just weeks ago, the police commissioner and the budget director testified right here in this room that these officers were fully funded and critical to keeping violent crime down.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: To pull the rug out at the 11th hour after formal negotiations concluded and when it was too late to negotiate an alternative, it's a slap in the face to this body and an insult to the public trust.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: I can't understand how city government can find the time, the money, and the focus to name a bunch of stuff for a basketball team and change it all back, but we can't find a way to hire 580 more cops?

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Mayor Mamdani has proven himself to be a totally dishonest broker.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Good government requires transparency and baseline integrity, not governing by surprise behind closed doors.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: As proud as I am of our speaker, I can't support an expense budget handed down by an administration that negotiates in bad faith and compromises the safety of our streets.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: With that, I vote aye on all with the exception of Intro 31A, Intro 92A, Reso 529, Resos 530, 531, 532,

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Preconsidered Resolutions 534 and 535, Preconsidered Resolutions 539 and 540 and M69, Preconsidered Resolutions 543 and M74,

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: To the resolution 546 and M82.

utterances.speaker: Frank Morano
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilman.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: There is no perfect budget, and we're still in the work in progress.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: So to start, I have to say thank you to Speaker Menon and the leadership, and of course the mayor that shook their hands, but there's a lot more to work on.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: And I have to say thank you to Jonathan and all the finance team.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse

utterances.text: Thank you for not ignoring me when I talk, because there's needs in the community.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Michela, Michelena, and Nicole in the office, I want to say thank you.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: And to all the speakers'team, thank you.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: I'm looking forward to work on getting a rec center, but it's not possible as yet.

utterances.text: Thank you again.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: The speaker worked very hard in all of us, in all the team, in all my colleagues.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: City FEP that we talk so much about, 175.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: So thank you, Speaker Menon.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Businesses, we have a space for you.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Speaker Menon, understand that.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Mental health is very important.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Now so many baseline parks.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Deaf is a problem in our community.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Libraries, veterans, we address all those in this budget.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Fair, fair, right?

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: I have participated in so many people come out to say thank you all the time that we're able to provide the support.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Seniors are not left out in this budget.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: We cover people's needs.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Healthcare was present in that budget.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: So all I'm saying is not perfect.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: We have a lot more work to do, but I want to say thank you to all the leadership that come up from the speaker's aspect and the mayor's part.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Thank you for all the name, the street co-naming in my district.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: And Marley, thank you for our advocacy to bring it to my attention.

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: So to all chair, thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Your vote?

utterances.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
utterances.text: Sorry, I would I.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu

utterances.text: So please stay.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Palladino votes no on the budget, calling it the largest single-year spending increase in history dependent on new taxes, pension deferrals, and bailouts. She criticizes chasing out businesses and the middle class. Abreu cuts her off for time, and she votes no on budget and finance items, aye on the rest.
chapters.title: Palladino explains vote - voting no, fiscal concerns over spending and taxes

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Palladino.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: I've been a member of the City Council for nearly five years, and until now, I voted yes on every city budget.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: None of these budgets were perfect.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Each of them contained many things I disagreed with.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: But this isn't about demanding perfection.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: It's about making responsible decisions and choices that lay in front of us.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Unfortunately, this year is different.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: This year I have to vote no.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Even by the standards of our dysfunctional city government, this budget represents a complete departure from the fiscal reality.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: It spends more money than New York City has ever spent at precisely the moment that we could least afford it.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: This is the largest single year increase in spending history.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: It depends on new taxes from Albany, pension deferrals, budget bailouts from the governor, and even the revenue projections are optimistic.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: This isn't a balanced budget.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: This is budgeted by a bailout.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: It's a ticking time bomb.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: And the beginning of a fiscal death.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: spiral that our current leadership will not be able to pull us out of because we lack both the experience and the seriousness in which to do so.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: What will we do next year and then the year after that?

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: The only sustainable way to support a higher spending is through sustained economic growth by retaining the supporting businesses, encouraging private investment, and expanding our tax base.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Instead, we are doing the exact opposite.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: We are actively chasing out the very people we need most with political stunts and threats that alienate entire sectors of our economy while we nickel and dime the working class people.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: The middle class, the financial sector, the businesses of all sizes are choosing to go elsewhere, and they are being replaced with low-income foreigners and transplants that require significant subsidy

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: just to survive.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: No, we waited five hours.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: No, Councilmember.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: I'm sorry.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Let me just finish up my one thing.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: One thing.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Let me get this out.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Okay?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Councilmember, your time is up.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Please tell us how you're going to vote.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: My time is up.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: We waited five hours to speak today.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Please mute her mic.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Please mute her mic.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Let me just say this.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: We need simple things.

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: I've been consistent with everyone tonight.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Very consistent.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Please keep it down in the gallery.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: If not, you have to be removed.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Please keep it down in the chambers, please.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Councilmember Palladino, how are you voting, Councilmember?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Councilmember Palladino, how are you voting?

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: Oh, no, and I want to vote no on intro 31A, intro 92A, reso 529, resos 530, 531, 532,

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: preconsidered resos 534, 535, preconsidered resos 539, 540, M69, preconsidered resos 543, M74, preconsidered reso 544, M80,

utterances.speaker: Vickie Paladino
utterances.text: preconsidered resos 545, M81, preconsidered resos 546, and M82.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Restler discloses OMB (wife) and nonprofit (mother) associations. He credits Mayor Mamdani for an honest accounting of financial obligations and securing Albany funding. He celebrates FEPS but notes NYCHA and supportive housing vacancies remain too high. Votes aye.
chapters.title: Restler explains vote - mayor's honest budget, FEPS, NYCHA vacancies remain

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Councilmember Ressler.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: Great, thank you.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: I will take the opportunity to engage from there.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: I actually think that this mayor deserves credit for an honest accounting of our financial obligations.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: When he came into office after years of corruption and cronyism,

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: He gave a real analysis of the actual costs that we're facing as a city and presented an honest budget.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: And then went up to Albany and actually secured the billions of dollars that were needed to present a balanced executive budget.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: It was...

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: An impressive performance.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: I'm grateful to the mayor and the speaker and our finance chair and of course the council finance staff and the OMB staff for all the hard work that went in to this adopted budget.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: You know, there are many good things in here for us to celebrate.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: As always, there's more money for parks and more money for CUNY and more money for libraries and more money for cultural.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: And lots of things like fair fairs that I'm really proud to see us investing in.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: But most notably, our accomplishment on FEPS, led by Councilmember Pierina-Sanchez and supported by our speaker and this body and this body.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: and many great advocates, was a remarkable achievement that will help tens of thousands of families afford to live in New York City.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: We didn't get everywhere we wanted to on NYCHA vacant units, where there are more than 6,000 of them today, and there are going to be a similar number a year from now.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: We didn't get everywhere we wanted to go on vacant supportive housing.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: There's still going to be...

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: Too many a year from now.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: But there were many good investments in this budget and for which I am proud to vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Council, remember your disclosures, please.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: And on my disclosures,

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: The Mayor's Office of Management and Budget is funded in this budget, and I'm very proud of my wife who works there.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: The Mercantile Library Association of the City of New York and the Brooklyn Heights Association are funded in this budget, and I'm proud of my mom who serves on the board of both of those organizations.

utterances.speaker: Lincoln Restler
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilmember.

chapters.summary: Council Member Riley discloses multiple associations. He praises the budget for lowering costs, expanding opportunity, and strengthening services, highlighting NYC Kids Rise and housing stability investments. Votes aye on all except no on 92A.
chapters.title: Riley explains vote - NYC Kids Rise, housing stability, fiscal responsibility

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Councilmember Riley.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceeding that the Bronx Public Administrator's Office, the New York City's Department of Education, Girl Scouts of Greater New York, the YMCA, the New York City Administration for Children's Services, and MCNY are funded in the budget.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: We are adopting my spouse, children, business associate, parent, sibling, and I respectfully are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: Majority leader, may I explain?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: Today I'm proud to vote to support this budget.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: Throughout this process, I've been proud to work with Speaker Menon, Finance Chair Linda Lee, my council colleagues, Mayor Mamdani and his administration, and most importantly, our finance team.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: We deliver the fiscally responsible budget that lowers the cost of living, expands opportunity, and strengthens the essential services New Yorkers rely on each and every single day.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: This budget reflects our shared commitment to invest in people and create pathways to opportunity, from helping families build generational wealth through expanding college saving account,

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: for the public school students to make a critical investment that strengthens housing stability and supports communities across our city.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: Budgets are a reflection of our values, and this agreement demonstrates what is possible when we work together with purpose.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: I'm proud to support this budget that invests in working families while laying a stronger foundation for a more affordable, equitable, and prosperous New York City tomorrow.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: And with that being said, I vote aye on all with the exception of 92A, which I'm going to know.

utterances.speaker: Kevin C. Riley
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Salaam discloses YMCA association. He thanks the mayor, Finance Chair Lee, and Speaker Menin for delivering a budget that puts New Yorkers first, highlighting the CityFEPs settlement and largest Fair Fares expansion. Votes aye.
chapters.title: Salaam explains vote - CityFEPs, Fair Fares, responsible negotiation

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Salaam.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: I'm just loading on the record of the council proceedings that the YMCA is funded in this budget that we are adopting and that my family and I are associated with that not-for-profit.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: Thank you for the opportunity to share the pride that I feel for the efforts of my colleagues.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: I want to thank the mayor, and I also want to thank our chair of finance, Linda Lee, and the entire council team.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: But I want to especially thank our speaker, Julie-Menin.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: Their hard work and dedication delivered a budget that puts New Yorkers first.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: Their leadership helped secure a historic agreement to expand City FEPS, ending years of litigation, and creating a stronger, more sustainable program that will help keep more New Yorkers safely housed for years to come.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: This budget also includes the largest, in fact, expansion of fare fares in the program's history, increasing, as has been said,

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: eligibility to 200% of the federal poverty level and providing an additional 340,000 New Yorkers with access to half-price subway and bus fares.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: These accomplishments reflect the Council's leadership's ability to negotiate,

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: and responsibly, reaching agreements that are truly in the best interest of our city while delivering meaningful results.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: For working class families.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: Again, congratulations to our speaker, Julie-Menin, Chair Linda-Lee, and every single person involved in making this budget a success.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: And I vote aye.

utterances.speaker: Yusef Salaam
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilman.

chapters.summary: Council Member Sanchez discloses CUNY and Neighborhood Defender Services associations. She thanks CityFEPs plaintiffs, the Speaker's team, Progressive Caucus leadership, OGC, Legal Aid Society, coalition partners, and her own staff and family. She also notes historic street vending reforms funding. Votes aye on all, abstaining on Intro 929 and 553.
chapters.title: Pierina Sanchez explains vote - thanks CityFEPs plaintiffs, advocates, and team

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Verena Sanchez.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the City University of New York and Neighborhood Defender Services is funded in the budget we are adopting, and my sibling and business associate, respectively, are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Majority Leader, permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: I want to take a moment to thank everyone involved in the suit, the budget on this City FEPS win for low-income and working-class New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: First, the plaintiffs, Mary Vincent, Caroline Tejada, Mary Cronin, and Susan Axe.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: The speakers team, Miguelina Camilo, my fellow Dominicana, Jonathan Zott, and Trit, and Bob Tribual, Simone Jones, the whole comms team, you all have gone in, Julia Agos, Ben Fang, Henry Robbins,

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: the Progressive Caucus, who really held me down and held us together, especially the leadership, council members, Caban, Nurse, Aviles, and Santos Suoso.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: OGC, those that fought this case and won in the courts, Maka Ejeber, our general counsel, Amanda Del Grosso, Jeff Campagna was up many nights.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: The ledge division, Andrea Vasquez, Mita Deshmuk, Jeff Baker, the finance team, especially Jonathan Rosenberg, Nick Connell, and Julia Haramis.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: The Legal Aid Society, our co-counselors, Judith Goldner, Robert Desir, Ed Josephson, the coalition, Vocal New York, the Homes Can't Wait.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Coalition, Vocal New York, WIN, Coalition for the Homeless, Volunteers of America, Safety Net Project, Community Service Society,

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: and my team, especially my Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy, Ben Ratner, and Deputy Chief for Communications and Public Engagement, Kim Castellano, but also Gerard, Dylan, Maria Jose,

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Soraya, Oriana, Maria, our chief, there's two of them, two Marias, our interns, Mia, Marcus, and Ari, and finally my family, my husband, Devin, and Yaya, and Lalo, thank you for sharing your mommy with New York City, thank you to my mom and dad,

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: my brother, Tio Cao, Tia Kaki, the Matthews, our neighbors, thank you for helping with childcare over the many weekends and late nights that we had to work.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: I'm thrilled about what we accomplished today.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: I'm thrilled about many other parts of the budget.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: The historic street vending reforms have money in this budget to be implemented in the next year.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Please wrap up.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Can you please tell me how you're voting?

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Yes, okay.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Just shout out to the PC.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: I vote aye on all with the exception of Intro 929 and 553 on which I have seen.

utterances.speaker: Pierina Ana Sanchez
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: My apologies, Council Member.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: No or abstain?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Abstain.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Oh, thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Santosuosso thanks the mayor and Speaker for the FEPS settlement. She expresses disappointment in NYCHA and supportive housing funding levels, celebrates baselined parks workers, libraries, child care, and immigration legal services. She votes no on 553 (duplicates state program) and 929 (First Amendment concerns), aye on the rest.
chapters.title: Santosuosso explains vote - FEPS, NYCHA vacancies, parks, small business

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Santos Suoso.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: I want to thank the mayor and speaker and definitely their respective staffs for landing the plane today.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: And in particular, I'm grateful that our FEPS lawsuit is dropped and that we're on a path to preventing thousands more evictions.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: I'm hopeful that in budgets to come, that number expands.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: And I'm grateful to the Progressive Caucus and Councilmember Pierina Sanchez for the leadership and commitment to FEPS.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: At the same time, I'm disappointed that other progressive caucus priorities did not make it to the levels of funding that they should have today, and in particular, funding to fill NYCHA vacancies and funding for expanding supportive housing, which constituents in my district would have benefited enormously from.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: I'm saddened that we're missing out on the urgent and important opportunity we had to house people faster.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: And so for those in my district facing eviction but unable to apply for city FEPS on a NYCHA waitlist or in need of supportive housing, we have not forgotten you and we will continue fighting for you.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: In positive news, I'm excited for the baselining of parks workers and hopeful that this puts us on a path to securing a baseline 1% of the budget for parks.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: I'm also thrilled at the funding for libraries, child care, and immigration legal services.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: This budget will also fund big projects in our district from new school gymnasiums and science labs to a new school gymnasium.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: improvements to the beloved Coney Island Aquarium.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: Additionally, we secured millions of dollars in funding for nonprofits to continue their critical work across the district.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: I have been inspired time and time again by the clarity, intelligence, and courage displayed by my colleagues throughout this process, especially the Progressive Caucus.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: We have a long road ahead of us to create a more affordable, equitable, and just city, but we also have leaders of conviction and integrity to lead the way.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: So I vote aye on the budget.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: On legislation, I proudly vote aye on 910A that will prevent thousands of New York City small business owners from having to pay upwards of $10,000 to replace their roll-down security gates.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: That's important to me as a small business owner.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: Thank you to Councilmember Althea Stevens for your swift action with this legislation.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: I'm voting no on 553 simply because there's already a state program that covers this that is underutilized.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: And I'm voting no on 929 because at a time when protest is being punished by this presidential administration, I don't want to ascribe my name to something that even hints at the possibility of curtailing First Amendment rights, though I'm grateful for all of the amendments to this legislation.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: And I on the remainder.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: Thank you all.

utterances.speaker: Kayla Santosuosso
utterances.text: Congratulations on the budget.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilmember.

chapters.summary: Council Member Schulman discloses Mayor's Office association. She highlights spay-neuter pilot, pet pantry, $10 million for Far Rockaway trauma center, $2.2 million in discretionary funding, and a Spider-Man Way co-naming in Forest Hills. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Schulman explains vote - health investments, Spider-Man Way, Far Rockaway trauma center

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Schulman.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the office of the mayor of the city of New York is funded in the budget we are adopting.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: My sibling is associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: Thank you, Majority Leader.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: Today I am proudly voting for one of the most robust and comprehensive budgets this council has passed in quite a while.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: I want to thank and congratulate Speaker Julie-Menin for spearheading

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: a budget that reflects what we value as a city.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: This budget makes important investments in our communities by providing housing for those who are homeless, tackling food insecurity,

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: enhancing our parks and libraries, supporting older adults, improving health and quality of life by keeping our neighborhoods safe and clean, and continuing to strengthen the programs New Yorkers rely on every day.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: As chair of the Committee on Health, I'm especially pleased that this budget includes investments in the Spay-Nuda pilot initiative I introduced last year and provides for a much-needed pet pantry program.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: I also want to thank the speaker for the $10 million investment in the Far Rockaway Trauma Center, something that is sorely needed.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: We need a lot more, but this is a great start, and it hasn't been done before, so I want to thank her for that.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: This year, I am privileged to have more than $2.2 million in discretionary funding to support local organizations in my district and more than $10 million in capital funding for...

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: To benefit my constituents, more details to follow on that, so stay tuned.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: Thank you again to Speaker Menon, and special thanks to the Speaker's negotiating team, Miguelina, Jonathan, and Nathan, Finance Chair Linda Lee, the City Council Finance Division, my colleagues and my wonderful staff, led by my Chief of Staff Jonathan Boucher and Kevin McAlee,

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: my...

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: Budget director for their hard work throughout this budget process.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: I also want to mention that I am, that I have a street name, co-naming for Spider-Man Way in Forest Hills for nearly six years.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: 65 years, Spider-Man has represented the very best of Queens.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: And actually, believe it or not, there is a Parker family that did live at 20 Ingram Street.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: So I'm very much looking forward to that.

utterances.speaker: Lynn C. Schulman
utterances.text: And with that, I vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilman.

chapters.summary: Council Member Stevens discloses Columbia Presbyterian and YMCA associations. She votes no on the budget, stating District 16 in the Bronx received little to no investments despite being one of the poorest districts. She invokes Dr. King on inequality and pledges continued advocacy. Votes no on budget, Intro 92A, 929A, and Reso 529.
chapters.title: Stevens explains vote - voting no, District 16 received inadequate investments

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Councilmember Stevens.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: I would like to disclose on the record of these council proceedings that Columbia Presbyterian and the YMCA are funded in the budget, and we are adopting in my child the civil, respectively, are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Our budget is more than numbers on a spreadsheet.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: It's a more document.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: It reflects who we value, who we prioritize, and whether we are willing to invest in communities that have been asked to do more for less for generations.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: I represent District 16 in the Bronx, one of the poorest districts in our city.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Yet, our district allocations were more than disappointing.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: And for the first time, we got little to no investments.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Given the level of investments, it's just inadequate.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: For the community facing deep challenges in housing, youth services, public safety, education, economic opportunities, the amount feels like crumbs.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Equality cannot simply mean a slogan.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Equality means people who need more should get more.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: If we truly believe in fairness, the community carrying the greatest burden should be the ones not left fighting over the smallest resources.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Dr.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Martin Luther King once said, our America who often have

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: been taking necessities from the masses to give luxury to the classes.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: God never intended for one group of people to live with wealth while others lived in poverty.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Those words still challenge us today.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: When I ran for office, it was not for a title.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: It was to ensure that our voice would be heard and that who are often overlooked.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: For families, young people, seniors, and working class residents who deserve more

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: than just symbolic support.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: It saddens me that I cannot tell my constituents that this budget fully meets the urgency of their needs.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: And while I respect the hard work of the negotiations, I must be honest where District 16 stands.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: So today I will be voting no on this budget, not because I've given up on my community, but because I believe we deserve better.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: And let's be clear, the Sweet 16, we're resilient.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: We will continue to organize, advocate, raise our voices into our community, receive the investments, dignity, and opportunity we've always deserved.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: I will also be voting, I will be voting no on Intro 92A, Intro 929A, and Reso 529.

utterances.speaker: Althea V. Stevens
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilman.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thomas Henry.

utterances.speaker: Shanel Thomas-Henry
utterances.text: I am disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the Learning Tree STEM Arts School, Jamaica Center for Arts and Learning, and Edge School of the Arts are funded in the budget we are adopting, and my children are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Shanel Thomas-Henry
utterances.text: With that being said, I vote aye on all.

chapters.summary: Council Member Ung discloses YMCA association. She praises the budget for parks, housing vouchers, vendor enforcement, and especially the $53 million NYC Kids Rise investment, which gives every child regardless of immigration status a fairer chance at college. Votes aye.
chapters.title: Ung explains vote - NYC Kids Rise, immigrant families, vendor enforcement

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the YMCA is funded in the budget we are adopting and I'm associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote?

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: The budget we are voting today makes real impactful investments in community across New York.

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: Under the leadership of Speaker Menon, we have negotiated a budget that funds our parks, expand access to housing vouchers to families in need, and an increase in resources for vendor enforcement.

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: I know I speak for parents in my district when I say that I'm particularly excited for the $53 million investment in New York City Rise secured in this budget.

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: In immigrant communities like the one that I represent, this program gives every child, regardless of their family's income or immigrant status, a fairer chance at college and vocational training.

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: There are many families who might not be familiar with accounts like 529s, but this shows them that they have available options to save for college.

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: I also want to thank Chair Lee, the Speaker's team, and the Finance team for all your hard work.

utterances.speaker: Sandra Ung
utterances.text: The dot, I vote aye.

She highlights historic investments in cultural institutions, libraries, homeowners, unhoused New Yorkers, and racial equity. She thanks Speaker Menin, Chair Lee, the administration, and the entire finance staff. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Williams explains vote - cultural sector, libraries, racial equity investments

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Williams.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: I am disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the Children's Rescue Fund, the City University of New York, and Queens Alumni Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Incorporated are funded in the budget we are adopting and my sibling and I

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: respectively are associated with those entities.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: So today's vote reflects a shared commitment to making

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: investments that strengthen our neighborhoods, support working families, and expand opportunity across New York City.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: This year's budget includes much needed and historic investments in the cultural sector, our libraries, for homeowners, for those folks who are unhoused, and to also advancing racial equity.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: Our responsibility doesn't end with passing a budget.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: It continues with delivering on these investments and ensuring New Yorkers see the impact.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: And I just wanted to end also with a round of thank yous.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: So I want to thank Speaker Julie-Menin for all her hard work, Chair Lee, the administration.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: And Chair Lee mentioned all of the amazing finance staff that I bother so much.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: And I don't want to go through the list again, but all the folks that she mentioned, thank you all so very much.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: Some I have even more conversations with, some I have less, but I just appreciate you all for dealing with me and for all of your hard work during this year's budget and working so hard behind the scenes to bring everything together.

utterances.speaker: Nantasha M. Williams
utterances.text: And with that, I vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Wilson celebrates his first budget vote. He highlights CityFEPs, Fair Fares, NYC Kids Rise, and $86 million for immigrant legal services, referencing the wrongful ICE detention of council staff member Rafael. He thanks Speaker Menin, Chair Lee, and Mayor Mamdani. Votes aye on all.
chapters.title: Wilson explains vote - first budget, ICE detention, immigrant legal services

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Wilson.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: Permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: I'm excited to vote yes on this year's budget, my first one.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: I know I just got here a few weeks ago, so I can't take credit for everything, but I do want to point to some of the bigger wins in the budget.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: This budget comes at a time when the Trump administration is taking health care away from the neediest, violating the rights of transgender New Yorkers, trying to instill fear in our immigrant communities, and doing incredible damage to the cultural, social, and legal fabric of our country.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: This budget, by contrast, demonstrates our city's values by fighting back and representing a more hopeful and humane alternative.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: There are aspects of this budget that are a long time coming that are huge wins.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: The expansion of City FEPS is an incredible win for the city, and I want to congratulate Councilmember Pierina Sanchez for her tireless work on this initiative.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: The expansion of fare fares, which is going to provide access and reliability to transit for thousands of New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: And we've got big news for New York's cutest, with every New York City public school kindergartner now receiving a college savings account, which will help families build wealth, expand opportunity, and invest in the next generation.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: We here at the City Council just welcome back to our co-worker, Rafael, who was wrongfully detained by ICE for over five months.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: We fought hard for him and have been outspoken about the atrocities of ICE.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: In this budget, we made sure to back up our...

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: our word by providing over 86 million for our service providers to continue providing legal support for our immigrant neighbors in need.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: I want to thank Speaker Julie-Menin for her leadership in holding firm on the Council's priorities, Finance Committee Chair Linda Lee for her expertise, and Mayor Mondami for his partnership, as well as advocates and all of my colleagues here.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: While no budget solves every New York problem, I believe we are passing a budget that supports all members of the District 3 community and brings us closer to a more equitable, fair, and progressive New York City.

utterances.speaker: Carl Wilson
utterances.text: And with that, I vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: One.

utterances.speaker: Julie Won
utterances.text: I want to disclose on the record that my son attends an early childhood education center, 3K, from the Department of Education in my district, and I vote no on Intro 929A, Intro 553A, and aye on the rest.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Wong.

utterances.speaker: Phil Wong
utterances.text: I vote yes and no except for intro 31, intro 92, and pre-consider resolutions 530, 531, 532, 534, and 535.

utterances.speaker: Phil Wong
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Joann.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the city of council proceeding that

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: the New York City Park, New York City Department of Education and the Jewish Community House Multiculture Centers tell us

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: Toledo scope.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola

utterances.speaker: Council Staff

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: When I become a chair of committee on aging, I made the promise.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: And I just also, I'm very happy to see our library is fully funded and our kids have a place for seven days reading.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: She

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: I want to shout out to our speaker Julie-Menin, finance chair Linda-Lee and the finance team who champion every one of those fights alongside with us and you guys did a wonderful job.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: And also I will say no budget is perfect.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: No one will be 100%

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: satisfied, but I think this is a good budget and we have more work to do, but we will keep fighting for New York City.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Joann Ariola
utterances.text: Oh, and I vote aye on the budget and everything except I want to abstain, Razo 534 and 535.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilman.

chapters.summary: Minority Leader Carr discloses multiple Staten Island associations. He thanks the Speaker for historic Staten Island investments, parks, libraries, and Rainy Day Fund, but votes no on the expense budget, condemning the last-minute removal of 580 NYPD officers as bad faith by the administration. Votes no on budget and finance items, aye on the rest.
chapters.title: Carr explains vote - Staten Island investments, voting no over stripped NYPD funding

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Carr.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: I'm disclosing on the record of the council proceedings that the Italian Club of Staten Island, the Jewish Community Center of Staten Island, and the Garibaldi-Miucci Museum are funded in the budget we are adopting, and I am associated with those organizations.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And New York City Health and Hospitals is funded in the budget we are adopting, and my spouse is associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: Majority Leader, permission to explain my vote.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff
utterances.text: Permission granted.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: I want to begin by thanking the folks in this room who are happiest that the budget process is concluding, and that's the staff.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: In particular, my team, Peter Spencer, Loren Centineo, my hardworking budget director, and my chief of staff, Giuseppe Desario, the Speaker's Office, Miguelina and Jonathan, and the Finance Division, led by Nathan.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: I thank Chair Lee for mentioning all the names earlier because they deserve to be said aloud for all the great work and hard hours that they put in.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: I also really want to thank the speaker for all of her support in this budget.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: She set out to really disprove the notion that Staten Island is the forgotten borough.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And wearing my Staten Island delegation hat for a moment, I have to thank her for the incredible investment that she has made in Staten Island.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: It's probably the largest single-year investment in recent memory.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And she is really showing that.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: her intentions turned into action and I can't thank her enough for everything she's going to be doing to improving the quality of life in Staten Island.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: There are also huge wins in this budget that could not have happened but for her leadership and the advocacy of the council

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: that are important not only to Staten Island but all boroughs, cultural, libraries, and investments in our parks departments so that our parkies can better take care of our open spaces and recreation areas.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: Actual money being put away for savings in the rating day fund.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: We are light years away from where we were earlier in the year when the rainy day fund was being rated and we were threatened with property tax increases of nine and a half percent.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: Thanks to the leadership of this council and in particular our speaker that never came to pass and there are so many things to champion in this budget.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: All budgets are full of things we love and full of things that we do not love.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And I've talked about some of the ones that I love just now, but one of the things that I do not love about this budget is one in particular that looms large over this moment is the missing funding for 580 new police officers.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: Earlier this year, I was pleasantly surprised when the administration indicated that it was promising to expand the NYPD uniform headcount by 580 officers.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: For someone who's been trying to get us back to peak service levels over the course of my tenure, this was good news.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And it was one that I was going to be taking back home to my constituents as a reason for supporting this budget.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: A budget like everything else contains things that they will love and things that they will not.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: consistently what I've seen over the course of my tenure in government, whether it's now as a member or before as a member of staff, is that the NYPD is increasingly asked to do less with less.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And I am a fiscal conservative.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: I always want to see if we could be creative and do more with less.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: But the standard that is being imposed on the NYPD is unsustainable.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: They are a cornerstone of our civil service.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And the decision at the last minute, at the 11th hour, as my South Shore colleague was describing earlier, to pull away this funding, to say that it is not needed, beggars belief.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And all the folks on the other side of the building in the mayor's office would need to do to realize that they are wrong about what is needed in terms of service levels at the NYPD is to talk to the rank and file.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: Talk to the labor unions that represent them.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: This is supposed to be a labor-friendly town where we defer to the expertise of our partners in labor, unless you're on the other side of the building and that labor organization wears a uniform.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: We need to make sure that the NYPD stops relying not only on overtime, but mandatory overtime that is demoralizing and fatiguing our law enforcement.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: They do not have enough officers to meet the demands that they have in an ordinary course of a year, both with increasing numbers of protests, with events like FIFA and unexpected good events like the Knicks celebrations

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: that we experienced, the parade and all the watch parties, and all the other things that happen that we do not anticipate in a year.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: There are not enough officers to meet our need.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: In Staten Island, we have fewer officers today than we had 30 years ago when we were half the size that we are.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: That is unsustainable, and that's why I cannot support the budget that is now missing this committed promise from the administration.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: And considering where we began with this budget process, where the mayor inflated a deficit, and that inflation was laid bare as false by the hard work of our council leadership, our speaker, our chair at the finance committee,

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: and now where we are today, where they're saying that we don't need the police officers that they promised.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: This is not the honest and good faith partner that we need in our budget process on the other side of the building.

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: So with that being said, I'll be voting no on introductions 31A, 92A, resolution 529, resolutions 530, 531, and 532,

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: pre-considered resolutions 534 and 535, pre-considered resolutions 539 and 540 with accompanying message M69,

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: pre-considered resolution 543 and M74, pre-considered resolution 544 and M80,

utterances.speaker: David M. Carr
utterances.text: pre-considered resolution 545 and M81, and pre-considered resolution 546 and M82, and I on the rest.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Minority leader, I believe you already made your disclosures.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: Okay, thank you.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: I would like to recognize Councilmember Selvena Brooks-Powers, who I believe has to make an additional disclosure.

utterances.speaker: Selvena N. Brooks-Powers
utterances.text: I'd like to also disclose that we are funding the YMCA, a not-for-profit, in this budget, and myself and my family are associated to that organization.

utterances.speaker: Shaun Abreu
utterances.text: I vote aye on all.

utterances.speaker: Council Staff

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I'd like to disclose on the record that the 92nd Street Y is funded in the budget we are adopting, and my child is associated with that entity.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I vote aye on all, and I know I did a lot of thank yous this morning in the budget handshake and earlier today in my speech, but...

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Once again, thank you to the hardworking finance team.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: You've gotten such little sleep.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And we would not be at this moment without you.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And I think it really bears noting that, you know, our finance team, so many of them have been here for many, many years.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: They work through multiple speakers, multiple mayors.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: It's not about who the speaker is, who the mayor is.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: It is about the work of helping New Yorkers.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And we owe you a debt of gratitude for everything you've done.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: And once again, thank you to my whole team who has just been tremendous.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Miguelina, Jonathan, Simone, John, Bob, our whole comms team, Henry, Jack.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: Yoav, who've literally worked, I mean, they've been helping all of the members.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I just,

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: I really can't thank them all enough.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: We so deeply appreciate the amazing work that you have done.

utterances.speaker: Julie Menin
utterances.text: So thank you.

chapters.summary: Council Member Maloney introduces the resolution establishing annual Knicks Day on June 13, celebrating the 2026 NBA championship and the resilience and pride the Knicks inspired across all five boroughs.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney
utterances.text: For 53 years, New Yorkers waited for this moment.

utterances.text: Across all five boroughs, neighbors gathered in living rooms, sports bars, and community spaces to watch the game.

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney

utterances.speaker: Virginia Maloney

summary: The Council adopted a historic $125.8 billion FY2027 budget with record capital and expense investments, including expanded CityFEPs housing vouchers, Fair Fares to 200% FPL, NYC Kids Rise $1,000 college savings for every kindergartner, and baselined parks funding.

The Council passed legislation on alternate side parking enforcement, office of child care creation, commercial organic waste expansion, child care background checks, NYPD security perimeter transparency, small business security systems, and security grill mandate repeal.

The Council approved 103 street co-namings, Knicks championship ceremonial resolutions, 250th anniversary commemorations, and a resolution opposing Trump's executive order restricting protections for transgender students.
title: Stated Meeting
